text: To get started with Northwind Cloud, create a workspace, then create your first project and add a board. The setup checklist on the home page walks you through inviting teammates, connecting an integration, and importing existing tasks from CSV. Most teams are up and running in under an hour.

Northwind Cloud has three member roles. Admins manage billing, members, and security settings. Members can create and edit projects and tasks. Viewers have read-only access. Invite teammates from Settings, Members, Invite, and assign a role; you can change a role at any time.

To set up single sign-on, an admin opens Settings, Security, SSO and chooses SAML or OpenID Connect. Paste your identity provider's metadata URL and certificate, then map the email attribute. Once SSO is enforced, members sign in through your identity provider. SSO is available on the Business and Enterprise plans.

To use the Northwind Cloud API, create an API key from Settings, Developer, API keys. Send it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header of each request. Keys are scoped to your workspace and can be revoked at any time. Treat a key like a password and never commit it to source control.

Two-factor authentication adds a second step at sign-in. Enable it from Settings, Security, Two-factor and scan the QR code with an authenticator app. Admins can require two-factor for the whole workspace. Save the recovery codes somewhere safe in case you lose your device.

Northwind Cloud sends notifications for mentions, assignments, and due dates. Choose in-app, email, or both from Settings, Notifications, and set a daily digest time so routine updates arrive in one email instead of many. You can mute a specific project without muting the rest.

Northwind Cloud integrates with Slack, GitHub, and Google Calendar. Connect an integration from Settings, Integrations. Slack posts board updates to a channel, GitHub links commits and pull requests to tasks, and Calendar syncs task due dates. Integrations are available on all paid plans.

You can export a workspace's data at any time from Settings, Data, Export, as CSV or JSON. Large exports are prepared in the background and a download link is emailed when ready. Northwind Cloud also keeps automatic daily backups of every workspace for disaster recovery.

You control your data. An admin can delete a workspace from Settings, Data, Delete workspace, which permanently removes all projects and tasks after a 14 day grace period. On request we provide a full data export and honor deletion requests to meet GDPR and similar rules.

The Northwind Cloud status page shows current system health and a history of past incidents. Subscribe to the status page to get an email when an incident opens or resolves. Planned maintenance is announced on the status page at least 48 hours in advance.

To change your plan, an admin opens Settings, Billing, Plan and selects a new tier. Upgrades take effect immediately and are prorated for the rest of the billing period; downgrades take effect at the next renewal. You can switch between monthly and annual billing at any time.

Northwind Cloud enforces a strong password policy: at least twelve characters with a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols, checked against known breached passwords. Sessions expire after a period of inactivity, and admins can force a sign-out of all sessions from the security settings.

Pour configurer l'authentification unique, un administrateur ouvre Parametres, Securite, SSO et choisit SAML ou OpenID Connect. Collez l'URL de metadonnees et le certificat de votre fournisseur d'identite. Le SSO est disponible sur les plans Business et Enterprise.

Pour utiliser l'API Northwind Cloud, creez une cle API depuis Parametres, Developpeur, Cles API, et envoyez-la comme jeton Bearer dans l'en-tete Authorization. Les cles sont limitees a votre espace de travail et peuvent etre revoquees a tout moment.
